pipeline.c(3) Library Functions Manual pipeline.c(3)

NAME

pipeline.c - Three-thread render pipeline implementation.

SYNOPSIS

Data Structures


struct pipeline_frame_t
struct frame_queue_s
struct session_pipeline_s

Typedefs


typedef struct frame_queue_s frame_queue_t

Functions


asciichat_error_t session_pipeline_create (session_capture_ctx_t *capture, session_display_ctx_t *display, session_pipeline_t **out)
asciichat_error_t session_pipeline_run_main (session_pipeline_t *pipeline, session_should_exit_fn should_exit, session_keyboard_handler_fn keyboard_handler, void *user_data)

Variables


bool g_snapshot_first_frame_rendered

Detailed Description

Three-thread render pipeline implementation.

Definition in file pipeline.c.

Typedef Documentation

typedef struct frame_queue_s frame_queue_t

Function Documentation

asciichat_error_t session_pipeline_create (session_capture_ctx_t * capture, session_display_ctx_t * display, session_pipeline_t ** out)

Create and start the capture thread and (if --render-file) encode thread.

Parameters

capture Capture context (must have real media_source_t for synchronous mode)
display Display context (may have render_file set for --render-file mode)
out Pointer to receive allocated pipeline context

Returns

ASCIICHAT_OK on success, error code otherwise

Note

The pipeline does NOT own capture or display — caller must keep them alive until session_pipeline_destroy() returns.

Definition at line 383 of file pipeline.c.

387 {
388     if (!capture || !display || !out) {
389         return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "session_pipeline_create: NULL argument");
390     }
391 
392     session_pipeline_t *p = SAFE_CALLOC(1, sizeof(*p), session_pipeline_t *);
393     p->capture = capture;
394     p->display = display;
395     p->display_queue = frame_queue_create(4, "display");
396     p->encode_queue = frame_queue_create(256, "encode");  // Large buffer for slow encoding
397 
398     // Check if display has render_file configured (will be checked in encode thread)
399     // We store a flag to know whether to enqueue frames for encoding
400     double render_fps = session_display_get_render_fps(display);
401     bool has_file = session_display_has_render_file(display);
402 
403     // Enable encode thread if render_file is available
404     // Note: has_file can be true even if render_file creation failed (ctx->render_file exists)
405     // OR if render_fps is explicitly set for playback speed control
406     p->has_render_file = render_fps > 0 || has_file;
407     log_info("[PIPELINE_CREATE] render_fps=%.1f, has_file=%d, has_render_file=%d",
408              render_fps, has_file ? 1 : 0, p->has_render_file ? 1 : 0);
409 
410     atomic_store_bool(&p->stop, false);
411     atomic_store_u64(&p->first_frame_ns, 0);
412 
413     // Start capture thread
414     if (asciichat_thread_create(&p->capture_tid, "pipeline_capture", pipeline_capture_thread, p) != 0) {
415         frame_queue_destroy(p->display_queue);
416         frame_queue_destroy(p->encode_queue);
417         SAFE_FREE(p);
418         return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INIT, "session_pipeline_create: failed to start capture thread");
419     }
420 
421     // Start encode thread only if render_file is active
422     if (p->has_render_file) {
423         if (asciichat_thread_create(&p->encode_tid, "pipeline_encode", pipeline_encode_thread, p) != 0) {
424             atomic_store_bool(&p->stop, true);
425             asciichat_thread_join(&p->capture_tid, NULL);
426             frame_queue_destroy(p->display_queue);
427             frame_queue_destroy(p->encode_queue);
428             SAFE_FREE(p);
429             return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INIT, "session_pipeline_create: failed to start encode thread");
430         }
431     }
432 
433     *out = p;
434     return ASCIICHAT_OK;
435 }

    asciichat_thread_join(), atomic_store_bool(),
    atomic_store_u64(), session_pipeline_s::capture,
    session_pipeline_s::capture_tid, session_pipeline_s::display,
    session_pipeline_s::display_queue,
    session_pipeline_s::encode_queue,
    session_pipeline_s::encode_tid, ERROR_INIT,
    ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, session_pipeline_s::first_frame_ns,
    session_pipeline_s::has_render_file, log_info,

    session_display_get_render_fps(),
    session_display_has_render_file(), SET_ERRNO, and
    session_pipeline_s::stop.


Referenced by session_render_loop().






asciichat_error_t session_pipeline_destroy
  (session_pipeline_t * pipeline)


Stop threads and free pipeline.




Waits for capture and encode threads to exit cleanly.




Parameters


pipeline Pipeline context (safe to call with
  NULL)


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK


Definition at line 543 of file pipeline.c.


543                                                                          {
544     if (!pipeline) return ASCIICHAT_OK;
545 
546     log_info("[PIPELINE] Destroying pipeline, waiting for threads...");
547 
548     // Signal threads to stop (may already be stopped)
549     atomic_store_bool(&pipeline->stop, true);
550 
551     // Wake up any threads blocked on condition variable waits
552     // so they can check the stop flag and exit immediately
553     // instead of waiting for timeouts (e.g., 100ms frame_queue_pop timeout)
554     if (pipeline->display_queue) {
555         mutex_lock(&pipeline->display_queue->mu);
556         cond_broadcast(&pipeline->display_queue->not_empty);
557         cond_broadcast(&pipeline->display_queue->not_full);
558         mutex_unlock(&pipeline->display_queue->mu);
559     }
560     if (pipeline->encode_queue) {
561         mutex_lock(&pipeline->encode_queue->mu);
562         cond_broadcast(&pipeline->encode_queue->not_empty);
563         cond_broadcast(&pipeline->encode_queue->not_full);
564         mutex_unlock(&pipeline->encode_queue->mu);
565     }
566 
567     // Drain and wait for capture thread with 1 second timeout
568     if (asciichat_thread_is_initialized(&pipeline->capture_tid)) {
569         int join_result = asciichat_thread_join_timeout(&pipeline->capture_tid, NULL, 1000 * NS_PER_MS_INT);
570         if (join_result != 0) {
571             log_warn("[PIPELINE] Capture thread join timed out or failed (result=%d)", join_result);
572         }
573     }
574 
575     // Drain and wait for encode thread with 1 second timeout
576     if (asciichat_thread_is_initialized(&pipeline->encode_tid)) {
577         int join_result = asciichat_thread_join_timeout(&pipeline->encode_tid, NULL, 1000 * NS_PER_MS_INT);
578         if (join_result != 0) {
579             log_warn("[PIPELINE] Encode thread join timed out or failed (result=%d)", join_result);
580         }
581     }
582 
583     // Flush queues to free any remaining frames
584     frame_queue_flush(pipeline->display_queue, free_frame_generic);
585     frame_queue_flush(pipeline->encode_queue, free_frame_generic);
586 
587     // Don't free queue structures - debug_sync monitoring thread may still be accessing
588     // the condition variables. Queues will be cleaned up with the pipeline structure.
589     SAFE_FREE(pipeline);
590 
591     log_info("[PIPELINE] Pipeline destroyed");
592     return ASCIICHAT_OK;
593 }






References ASCIICHAT_OK,
    asciichat_thread_is_initialized(),
    asciichat_thread_join_timeout(), atomic_store_bool(),
    session_pipeline_s::capture_tid, cond_broadcast(),
    session_pipeline_s::display_queue,
    session_pipeline_s::encode_queue,
    session_pipeline_s::encode_tid, log_info, log_warn,
    frame_queue_s::mu, mutex_lock, mutex_unlock,
    frame_queue_s::not_empty, frame_queue_s::not_full,
    NS_PER_MS_INT, SAFE_FREE, and
  session_pipeline_s::stop.


Referenced by session_render_loop().






asciichat_error_t session_pipeline_run_main
  (session_pipeline_t * pipeline, session_should_exit_fn
  should_exit, session_keyboard_handler_fn keyboard_handler, void *
  user_data)


Main thread loop: drives terminal output and keyboard
  handling.




Pops frames from display queue, converts to ASCII, writes to
    terminal. Handles snapshot delay timer and exit conditions.




Parameters


pipeline Pipeline context created by
  session_pipeline_create()


should_exit Callback to check if rendering should
  stop


keyboard_handler Optional keyboard handler callback


user_data Opaque data passed to callbacks


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success


Note


This function blocks until should_exit() returns
  true or capture ends. Signals capture/encode threads to stop before
  returning.


Definition at line 437 of file pipeline.c.


442 {
443     if (!pipeline || !should_exit) {
444         return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "session_pipeline_run_main: NULL argument");
445     }
446 
447     log_info("[PIPELINE_MAIN] Starting main thread loop");
448 
449     bool snapshot_mode = GET_OPTION(snapshot_mode);
450     bool snapshot_done = false;
451 
452     while (!should_exit(user_data) && !atomic_load_bool(&pipeline->stop) && !snapshot_done) {
453         bool exit_check = should_exit(user_data);
454         log_debug_every(NS_PER_SEC_INT, "[PIPELINE_DEBUG] should_exit=%d, stop=%d, snapshot_done=%d", exit_check, atomic_load_bool(&pipeline->stop), snapshot_done);
455 
456         uint64_t pop_time_ns = time_get_ns();
457         pipeline_frame_t *frame = (pipeline_frame_t *)frame_queue_pop(pipeline->display_queue, 1 * NS_PER_MS_INT);
458 
459         if (!frame) continue;  // timeout, check should_exit again
460 
461         // Validate frame structure exists before accessing fields
462         if (!frame_is_valid(frame)) {
463             log_error("Received invalid frame: corrupted or NULL");
464             free_frame(frame);
465             frame = NULL;
466             continue;
467         }
468 
469         // Check for EOF sentinel (NULL pixels)
470         if (!frame->pixels) {
471             // EOF sentinel
472             log_info("[PIPELINE_MAIN_EOF] Received EOF sentinel, stopping");
473             free_frame(frame);
474             frame = NULL;
475             break;
476         }
477 
478         // Log frame pop with timestamp
479         log_info("[PIPELINE_MAIN_POP] frame=%p, pixels=%p, w=%d, h=%d at %llu ns",
480                   (void *)frame, (void *)frame->pixels, frame->w, frame->h, (unsigned long long)pop_time_ns);
481 
482         // Check if help screen is active - if so, don't render ASCII frames
483         bool help_is_active = pipeline->display && keyboard_help_is_active(pipeline->display);
484 
485         if (!help_is_active) {
486             log_info("[PIPELINE_MAIN_RENDER] Converting frame to ASCII: %dx%d", frame->w, frame->h);
487             // Convert to ASCII
488             image_t tmp = { .w = frame->w, .h = frame->h, .pixels = (rgb_pixel_t *)frame->pixels };
489             char *ascii = session_display_convert_to_ascii(pipeline->display, &tmp);
490             free_frame(frame);
491             frame = NULL;
492 
493             if (ascii) {
494                 // Write ASCII to terminal
495                 session_display_write_ascii(pipeline->display, ascii);
496                 SAFE_FREE(ascii);
497             }
498         } else {
499             free_frame(frame);
500             frame = NULL;
501         }
502 
503         // Keyboard polling
504         if (keyboard_handler) {
505             keyboard_key_t key = keyboard_read_nonblocking();
506             if (key != KEY_NONE) {
507                 log_debug("PIPELINE_KEYBOARD: Received key=%d", key);
508                 keyboard_handler(NULL, (int)key, user_data);  // NULL capture ctx
509             }
510         }
511 
512         // Snapshot mode: check if elapsed time has reached snapshot_delay duration
513         // This check runs every iteration after first frame is rendered (when display.c sets g_snapshot_first_frame_rendered)
514         if (snapshot_mode && !snapshot_done) {
515             if (g_snapshot_first_frame_rendered && g_snapshot_first_frame_rendered_ns > 0) {
516                 double snapshot_delay = GET_OPTION(snapshot_delay);
517                 uint64_t now_ns = time_get_ns();
518                 uint64_t elapsed_ns = now_ns - g_snapshot_first_frame_rendered_ns;
519                 double elapsed_sec = (double)elapsed_ns / (double)NS_PER_SEC_INT;
520 
521                 if ((unsigned long)(elapsed_sec * 10) % 10 == 0 || elapsed_sec < 0.2) {
522                     log_info("[SNAPSHOT_PIPELINE] CHECK: elapsed=%.3f target=%.2f", elapsed_sec, snapshot_delay);
523                 }
524 
525                 // snapshot_delay=0 means exit after first frame
526                 // snapshot_delay>0 means wait that many seconds before exiting
527                 bool should_snapshot_exit = (snapshot_delay == 0.0) || (elapsed_sec >= snapshot_delay);
528 
529                 if (should_snapshot_exit) {
530                     log_info("[SNAPSHOT_PIPELINE] EXITING - elapsed=%.3f target=%.2f", elapsed_sec, snapshot_delay);
531                     snapshot_done = true;
532                 }
533             }
534         }
535     }
536 
537     log_info("[PIPELINE_MAIN] Main loop exiting, signaling threads to stop");
538     atomic_store_bool(&pipeline->stop, true);
539 
540     return ASCIICHAT_OK;
541 }






References ASCIICHAT_OK, atomic_load_bool(),
    atomic_store_bool(), session_pipeline_s::display,
    session_pipeline_s::display_queue, ERROR_INVALID_PARAM,
    frame, g_snapshot_first_frame_rendered,
    g_snapshot_first_frame_rendered_ns, GET_OPTION,
    keyboard_help_is_active(), keyboard_read_nonblocking(),
    log_debug, log_debug_every, log_error, log_info,
    NS_PER_MS_INT, NS_PER_SEC_INT, SAFE_FREE,
    session_display_convert_to_ascii(),
    session_display_write_ascii(), SET_ERRNO,
    should_exit(), session_pipeline_s::stop, and
    time_get_ns().
